classes.name: oarepo_workflows.base.StateChangedNotifier

methods.name: oarepo_workflows.base.StateChangedNotifier.__call__
documentation:
Notify about a state change.

```
param identity

identity of the user who initiated the state change

param record

record whose state changed

param previous_state

previous state of the record

param new_state

new state of the record

param args

additional arguments

param uow

unit of work

param kwargs

additional keyword arguments
```

A protocol for a state change notifier.

State changed notifier is a callable that is called when a state of a record changes,
for example as a result of a workflow transition.

classes.name: oarepo_workflows.base.WorkflowChangeNotifier

methods.name: oarepo_workflows.base.WorkflowChangeNotifier.__call__
documentation:

```
param identity

identity of the user who initiated the workflow change

param record

record whose workflow changed

param previous_workflow_id

previous workflow of the record

param new_workflow_id

new workflow of the record

param args

additional arguments

param uow

unit of work

param kwargs

additional keyword arguments
```

A protocol for a workflow change notifier.

Workflow changed notifier is a callable that is called
when a workflow of a record changes.

methods.name: oarepo_workflows.requests.policy.WorkflowRequestPolicy.applicable_workflow_requests
documentation:
Return a list of applicable requests for the identity and context.

```
param identity

Identity of the requester.

param context

Context of the request that is passed to the requester generators.

return

List of tuples (request_type_id, request) that are applicable for the identity and context.
```

Base class for workflow request policies.

Inherit from this class
and add properties to define specific requests for a workflow.

The name of the property is the request_type name and the value must be
an instance of WorkflowRequest.

Example:
    class MyWorkflowRequests(WorkflowRequestPolicy):
        delete_request = WorkflowRequest(
            requesters = [
                IfInState("published", RecordOwner())
            ],
            recipients = [CommunityRole("curator")],
            transitions: WorkflowTransitions(
                submitted = 'considered_for_deletion',
                approved = 'deleted',
                rejected = 'published'
            )
        )

documentation: Initialize the permission.

methods.name: oarepo_workflows.services.permissions.generators.FromRecordWorkflow._get_workflow_id
documentation:
Get the workflow id from the context.

If the record is passed, the workflow is determined from the record.
If the record is not passed, the workflow is determined from the input data.

If the workflow is not found, an error is raised.

```
param record

Record to get the workflow from.

param context

Context to get the workflow from.

return

Workflow id.

raises MissingWorkflowError

If the workflow is not found on the record/data.
```
noinspection PyMethodMayBeStatic

methods.name: oarepo_workflows.services.permissions.generators.FromRecordWorkflow._get_permissions_from_workflow
documentation:
Get the permissions policy from the workflow.

At first the workflow id is determined from the context.
Then the permissions policy is determined from the workflow configuration,
is instantiated with the action name and the context and the permissions
for the action are returned.

methods.name: oarepo_workflows.services.permissions.generators.FromRecordWorkflow.needs
documentation: Return needs that are generated by the workflow permission.

methods.name: oarepo_workflows.services.permissions.generators.FromRecordWorkflow.excludes
documentation: Return excludes that are generated by the workflow permission.

methods.name: oarepo_workflows.services.permissions.generators.FromRecordWorkflow.query_filter
documentation:
Return query filters that are generated by the workflow permission.

Note: this implementation in fact will be called from WorkflowRecordPermissionPolicy.query_filters
for each registered workflow type. The query_filters are then combined into a single query.

Permission delegating check to workflow.

The implementation of the permission gets the workflow id from the passed context
(record or data) and then looks up the workflow definition in the configuration.

The workflow definition must contain a permissions policy that is then used to
determine the permissions for the action.

methods.name: oarepo_workflows.services.permissions.generators.IfInState.query_filter
documentation: Apply then or else filter.

Generator that checks if the record is in a specific state.

If it is in the state, the then_ generators are used, otherwise the else_ generators are used.

Example:
    .. code-block:: python

        can_edit = [ IfInState("draft", [RecordOwners()]) ]

classes.name: oarepo_workflows.services.permissions.generators.SameAs

methods.name: oarepo_workflows.services.permissions.generators.SameAs.__init__
documentation:
Initialize the generator.

```
param permission_name

Name of the permission to delegate to. In most cases,
```

it will look like "can\_\<action>". A property with this name must exist on the policy
and its value must be a list of generators.

methods.name: oarepo_workflows.services.permissions.generators.SameAs._generators
documentation: Get the generators from the policy.
noinspection PyUnusedLocal

methods.name: oarepo_workflows.services.permissions.generators.SameAs.needs
documentation: Get the needs from the policy.

methods.name: oarepo_workflows.services.permissions.generators.SameAs.excludes
documentation: Get the excludes from the policy.

methods.name: oarepo_workflows.services.permissions.generators.SameAs.query_filter
documentation: Search filters.

Generator that delegates the permissions to another action.

Example:
    .. code-block:: python
        class Perms:
            can_create_files = [SameAs("edit_files")]
            can_edit_files = [RecordOwners()]

would mean that the permissions for creating files are the same as for editing files.
This works even if you inherit from the class and override the can_edit_files.

functions.name: oarepo_workflows.requests.requests.RecipientEntityReference
documentation:
Return the reference receiver of the workflow request with the given context.

Note: invenio supports only one receiver, so the first one is returned at the moment.
Later on, a composite receiver can be implemented.

```
param request

Workflow request.

param context

Context of the request.

return

Reference receiver as a dictionary or None if no receiver has been resolved.
```

Implementation note: intentionally looks like a class, later on might be converted into one extending from dict.
noinspection PyPep8Naming

class_variables.name: oarepo_workflows.requests.requests.WorkflowRequest.requesters
documentation: Generators that define who can submit the request.

documentation: Generators that define who can approve the request.

documentation: Events that can be submitted with the request.

documentation: Transitions applied to the state of the topic of the request.

class_variables.name: oarepo_workflows.requests.requests.WorkflowRequest.escalations
documentation: Escalations applied to the request if not approved/declined in time.

Workflow request definition.

The request is defined by the requesters and recipients.
The requesters are the generators that define who can submit the request. The recipients
are the generators that define who can approve the request.

classes.name: oarepo_workflows.requests.requests.WorkflowTransitions

methods.name: oarepo_workflows.requests.requests.WorkflowTransitions.__getitem__
documentation: Get the transition by name.

Transitions for a workflow request.

If the request is submitted and submitted is filled,
the record (topic) of the request will be moved to state defined in submitted.
If the request is approved, the record will be moved to state defined in approved.
If the request is rejected, the record will be moved to state defined in rejected.

classes.name: oarepo_workflows.requests.requests.WorkflowRequestEscalation
documentation:
Escalation of the request.

If the request is not approved/declined/cancelled in time, it might be passed to another recipient
(such as a supervisor, administrator, ...). The escalation is defined by the time after which the
request is escalated and the recipients of the escalation.

methods.name: oarepo_workflows.requests.generators.recipient_generator.RecipientGeneratorMixin.reference_receivers

Return the reference receiver(s) of the request.

This call requires the context to contain at least "record" and "request_type"

Must return a list of dictionary serialization of the receivers.

Might return empty list or None to indicate that the generator does not
provide any receivers.

functions.name: oarepo_workflows.ext.finalize_app
documentation:
Finalize the application.

This function registers the auto-approve service in the records resources registry.
It is called from invenio_base.api_finalize_app entry point.

```
param app

Flask application
```

functions.name: oarepo_workflows.ext.patch_request_permissions
documentation:
Replace invenio request permissions.

If permissions for requests are the plain invenio permissions,
replace those with workflow-based ones. If user set their own
permissions, keep those intact.

noinspection PyMethodMayBeStatic

methods.name: oarepo_workflows.ext.OARepoWorkflows.init_services
documentation: Initialize workflow services.

methods.name: oarepo_workflows.ext.OARepoWorkflows.state_changed_notifiers
documentation:
Return a list of state changed notifiers.

State changed notifiers are callables that are called when a state of a record changes,
for example as a result of a workflow transition.

They are registered as entry points in the group `oarepo_workflows.state_changed_notifiers`.

methods.name: oarepo_workflows.ext.OARepoWorkflows.workflow_changed_notifiers
documentation:
Return a list of workflow changed notifiers.

Workflow changed notifiers are callables that are called when a workflow of a record changes.
They are registered as entry points in the group `oarepo_workflows.workflow_changed_notifiers`.

methods.name: oarepo_workflows.ext.OARepoWorkflows.set_state
documentation:
Set a new state on a record.

```
param identity

identity of the user who initiated the state change

param record

record whose state is being changed

param new_state

new state to set

param args

additional arguments

param uow

unit of work

param commit

whether to commit the change

param kwargs

additional keyword arguments
```

methods.name: oarepo_workflows.ext.OARepoWorkflows.set_workflow
documentation:
Set a new workflow on a record.

```
param identity

identity of the user who initiated the workflow change

param record

record whose workflow is being changed

param new_workflow_id

new workflow to set

param args

additional arguments

param uow

unit of work

param commit

whether to commit the change

param kwargs

additional keyword arguments
```

methods.name: oarepo_workflows.ext.OARepoWorkflows.get_workflow_from_record
documentation:

```
param record

record to get the workflow from. Can pass either a record or its parent.
```
noinspection PyMethodMayBeStatic

methods.name: oarepo_workflows.ext.OARepoWorkflows.record_workflows
documentation: Return a dictionary of available record workflows.

methods.name: oarepo_workflows.ext.OARepoWorkflows.default_workflow_events
documentation:
Return a dictionary of default workflow events.

Default workflow events are those that can be added to any request.
The dictionary is taken from the configuration key `DEFAULT_WORKFLOW_EVENTS`.

methods.name: oarepo_workflows.ext.OARepoWorkflows.get_workflow
documentation:
Get the workflow for a record.

```
param record

record to get the workflow for

raises MissingWorkflowError

if the workflow is not found

raises InvalidWorkflowError

if the workflow is invalid
```
